Sortable Engine — drag to reorder

Headless reorder: the engine tracks the drag, computes the order, and emits state. This page renders every list, card, gap and lifted clone.

Reorder by pointer-drag of on-page items — NOT file drag-and-drop (that is the separate drag-n-drop-engine). The engine reorders no DOM and applies no transforms; it emits the key order and the consumer paints.

1 · Vertical list — drag or keyboard

Drag a row to reorder; the gap opens where it will land and the others slide (consumer FLIP). Or focus a row and use the keyboard: Space grab, ↑/↓ move, Space drop, Esc cancel. A short click is never hijacked.

2 · Kanban board — connected columns

Drag cards within a column or across columns; the empty Done column is a valid drop target. With the keyboard, ←/→ move a grabbed card between columns.

To do

In progress

Done

3 · Drag handles

Only the ⠿ grip starts a drag — the body text stays selectable and a click on it is not a drag. The Pinned header is disabled (can't be picked up) and locked (non-displaceable): other rows can't move above it. Keyboard still works on every focusable row.

4 · Auto-scroll

Pick up a row and drag toward the top or bottom edge of this short viewport — the engine scrolls it so you can drop onto off-screen rows, and the drop index stays correct against the new scroll offset.

“changed” events — the consumer’s data-sync hook

On every commit the engine emits { key, fromList, fromIndex, toList, toIndex }. A real app updates its model here. Latest first:

No reorders yet — drag or keyboard a row above.
